Package com.shanebeestudios.vf.api

Class TileManager

java.lang.Object
com.shanebeestudios.vf.api.TileManager
public class TileManager extends Object
Manager for Tiles and VirtualChunks

  • Constructor Details

    • TileManager

      public TileManager(VirtualFurnaceAPI virtualFurnaceAPI)

  • Method Details

    • load

      public void load()

    • saveTile

      public void saveTile(@NotNull @NotNull Tile<?> tile, boolean saveToFile)
      Save a tile to YAML storage

      NOTE: If choosing not to save to file, this change will not take effect in the YAML file, this may be useful for saving a large batch and saving file at the end of the batch change, use saveConfig() to save all changes to file

      Parameters:
      tile - Tile to save
      saveToFile - Whether to save to file

    • saveAllTiles

      public void saveAllTiles()
      Save all current Tiles to file

    • saveConfig

      public void saveConfig()
      Save current tile YAML from RAM to file

    • getChunks

      public Collection<VirtualChunk> getChunks()
      Get all VirtualChunks
      Returns:
      Collection of all VirtualChunks

    • getLoadedChunks

      public Collection<VirtualChunk> getLoadedChunks()
      Get all loaded VirtualChunks
      Returns:
      Unmodifiable collection of all loaded VirtualChunks

    • loadChunk

      public boolean loadChunk(@NotNull @NotNull VirtualChunk chunk)
      Load a chunk
      Parameters:
      chunk - Chunk to load
      Returns:
      True if chunk was loaded, false if it did not load

    • unloadChunk

      public boolean unloadChunk(@NotNull @NotNull VirtualChunk chunk)
      Unload a chunk

      If the chunk has a ticket, it will not unload

      Parameters:
      chunk - Chunk to unload
      Returns:
      True if unloaded, false if it did not unload

    • isChunkLoaded

      public boolean isChunkLoaded(@NotNull @NotNull VirtualChunk chunk)
      Check if a chunk is loaded
      Parameters:
      chunk - Chunk to check
      Returns:
      True if loaded, false if not loaded

      z - Z coordinate of Chunk

      public Tile<?> getTile(@NotNull @NotNull Block block)
      Get a Tile at a specific Block
      Parameters:

      Returns:
      Tile if Tile exists at Block, otherwise null

    • getTile

      public Tile<?> getTile(int x, int y, int z, @NotNull @NotNull World world)
      Get a Tile at a specific location
      Parameters:
      x - X coord of Tile
      y - Y coord of Tile
      z - Z coord of Tile
      world - World of Tile
      Returns:
      Tile if Tile exists at location, otherwise null

    • removeTile

      public boolean removeTile(@NotNull @NotNull Tile<?> tile)
      Remove a Tile

      Will remove the tile from the VirtualChunk and yaml file.

      Parameters:
      tile - Tile to remove
      Returns:
      True if Tile was successfully removed

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(@NotNull @NotNull Block block, @NotNull @NotNull Furnace furnace)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block

      NOTE: DO NOT use FurnaceManager.createFurnace(String) methods in this part, instead create a new Furnace object to prevent double ticking/saving.

      Parameters:
      block - Block to create new FurnaceTile at
      furnace - Furnace to add to this tile
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(@NotNull @NotNull Block block, @NotNull @NotNull Furnace furnace, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<FurnaceTile> furnaceTileConsumer)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block

      NOTE: DO NOT use FurnaceManager.createFurnace(String) methods in this part, instead create a new Furnace object to prevent double ticking/saving.

      Parameters:
      block - Block to create new FurnaceTile at
      furnace - Furnace to add to this tile
      furnaceTileConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this tile
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(@NotNull @NotNull Block block, @NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties properties)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block
      Parameters:
      block - Block to create new FurnaceTile at
      name - Name of new Furnace
      properties - Properties of new Furnace
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(@NotNull @NotNull Block block, @NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties properties, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<FurnaceTile> furnaceTileConsumer)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block
      Parameters:
      block - Block to create new FurnaceTile at
      name - Name of new Furnace
      properties - Properties of new Furnace
      furnaceTileConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this tile
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block
      Parameters:
      x - X location of block
      y - Y location of block
      z - Z location of block
      world - World of block
      name - Name of new Furnace
      properties - Properties for new Furnace
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(int x, int y, int z, @NotNull @NotNull World world, @NotNull @NotNull String name, @NotNull @NotNull FurnaceProperties properties, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<Furnace> furnaceConsumer)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block
      Parameters:
      x - X location of block
      y - Y location of block
      z - Z location of block
      world - World of block
      name - Name of new Furnace
      properties - Properties for new Furnace
      furnaceConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this furnace
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(int x, int y, int z, @NotNull @NotNull World world, @NotNull @NotNull Furnace furnace)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block

      NOTE: DO NOT use FurnaceManager.createFurnace(String) methods in this part, instead create a new Furnace object to prevent double ticking/saving.

      Parameters:
      x - X location of block
      y - Y location of block
      z - Z location of block
      world - World of block
      furnace - Furnace to add to this tile
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(int x, int y, int z, @NotNull @NotNull World world, @NotNull @NotNull Furnace furnace, @NotNull @NotNull Consumer<Furnace> furnaceConsumer)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block

      NOTE: DO NOT use FurnaceManager.createFurnace(String) methods in this part, instead create a new Furnace object to prevent double ticking/saving.

      Parameters:
      x - X location of block
      y - Y location of block
      z - Z location of block
      world - World of block
      furnace - Furnace to add to this tile
      furnaceConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this furnace
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile

    • createFurnaceTile

      public FurnaceTile createFurnaceTile(int x, int y, int z, @NotNull @NotNull World world, @NotNull @NotNull Furnace furnace, Consumer<Furnace> furnaceConsumer, Consumer<FurnaceTile> furnaceTileConsumer)
      Create a new FurnaceTile attached to a block

      NOTE: DO NOT use FurnaceManager.createFurnace(String) methods in this part, instead create a new Furnace object to prevent double ticking/saving.

      Parameters:
      x - X location of block
      y - Y location of block
      z - Z location of block
      world - World of block
      furnace - Furnace to add to this tile
      furnaceConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this furnace
      furnaceTileConsumer - Consumer to manipulate this tile
      Returns:
      Instance of the new FurnaceTile
